title: Queen Forks Across the Rank
setup: Black's king and knight sit on the same 5th rank — the queen can splash both with one move.
hint: Slide the queen onto the 5th rank with check. From {{d5}}, she eyes the king and the knight at once.
explanation:
## The Idea

A **rank fork** by the queen. Black's king on a5 and knight on h5 share the 5th rank with nothing between them. The square {{d5}} is the perfect ambush — the queen attacks both pieces along the rank.

After {{Qd5+}} Black has no good answer:

- **Capture the queen?** The king on a5 is three squares away from d5 — too far.
- **Block the check?** The only Black piece left is the knight on h5, and from there it cannot jump to b5 or c5 (the only blocking squares) in a single move.
- **Run?** The king has to leave the 5th rank, which abandons the knight.

Black plays {{Kb6}} (or any king escape) and White grabs the knight with {{Qxh5}}.

## Pattern to Memorize

> When two enemy pieces stand on the **same rank, file, or diagonal**, ask whether your queen can reach a square between them — that's where the fork lives.
